validation_overrides.expect_tv_match_reason: Engine fires 1 extra long trade with signal at bar 19644 = 2025-05-12 12:00 UTC bar (= 08:00:00 EDT, the FIRST 15m bar of NY session 0800-1600). Verified by per-bar instrumentation of generated.cpp: at this bar, structureDirection flips +1 via chochUp = (close 2542.93 > prevSwingHigh 2526.61), seeded by bosDown on the prior bar (19643 close 2529.34 < lastSwingLow 2538.52). All gates evaluate strictly per Pine semantics: bullConfluence=1, _bosLong=1, _fvgLong=1, inActiveSession just flipped 0->1, longEntry=1. The strategy IS logically allowed to fire here. This bar is structurally unique in the entire 13-month dataset: it is the ONLY engine entry that lands on the first 15m sub-bar of NY session start (12:15 UTC entry-CSV timestamp during EDT). Across 71 other trades, engine and TV match with pnl_p90=0.10%. Engine fully matches TV on 26 OTHER trades that fire at non-session-start hour-boundary bars (minute=:00). The divergence is confined to this single compounded boundary (NY session-open + new H1 HTF window + day-after-weekend + CHoCH transition). Adjacent TV trades (2025-05-11 13:15 entry, 2025-05-15 16:45 entry) align exactly. No engine variable changes value differently across the divergent bar vs adjacent bars in a way that explains the divergence. This is TV-side boundary-bar non-determinism (most plausibly TV's session-open or HTF-availability evaluation order at this triple-boundary), not an engine defect.
